summary: Implemented the Lipstick swing and Star Rod swing fighter action callbacks as thin wrappers around existing shared sword/item swing helper routines, producing 8 new function matches and +272 matched bytes according to the decomp.dev GALE01 report.
what_changed: Added ftCo_LipstickSwing_Anim, ftCo_LipstickSwing_IASA, ftCo_LipstickSwing_Phys, and ftCo_LipstickSwing_Coll in src/melee/ft/ftlipstickswing.c.
Added ftCo_StarRodSwing_Anim, ftCo_StarRodSwing_IASA, ftCo_StarRodSwing_Phys, and ftCo_StarRodSwing_Coll in src/melee/ft/ftstarrodswing.c.
The Anim, IASA, and Phys callbacks delegate directly to ftCo_800CD1BC, ftCo_800CD204, and ftCo_800CD278 respectively.
The Coll callbacks delegate to ftCo_800CD2C4 with ft_800CD31C as the collision/transition callback argument.
Refined several ft_0CD1.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void functions taking ftCo_GObj*, including ftCo_800CD2C4(ftCo_GObj*, void (*)(ftCo_GObj*)).
Added a baselib forward include to ftlipstickswing.h, likely to support GObj-related declarations.
smart_moves: Recognized that Lipstick swing and Star Rod swing action callbacks share the same behavior pattern as existing common swing helpers instead of writing bespoke logic.
Used small one-call wrapper functions, which is a common matching tactic for action-state callback files where the real behavior lives in shared routines.
Unlocked matching by replacing placeholder prototypes with typed function signatures, especially the callback parameter on ftCo_800CD2C4.
Kept the item-specific action callback names while routing implementation through common ftCo helper functions, preserving symbol clarity without duplicating behavior.
decomp_lessons: When multiple item action states have identical Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll behavior, check for shared ftCo helper routines before reconstructing logic locally.
Unknown prototypes can block or obscure simple matches; tightening UNK_PARAMS to concrete ftCo_GObj* signatures can be enough to make wrapper functions match.
Collision callbacks may require modeling a helper as taking a function pointer, not just a GObj, as shown by ftCo_800CD2C4(gobj, ft_800CD31C).
Empty or placeholder source files can sometimes be completed with a small set of forwarding callbacks once the common action pattern is identified.
naming_conventions: Item swing action callbacks use ftCo_<ItemName>Swing_<Phase>, for example ftCo_LipstickSwing_Anim and ftCo_StarRodSwing_Coll.
Callback phase suffixes follow the standard fighter action callback set: Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll.
Address comments in headers are preserved in the format /* 0CD1BC */ before function declarations.
Shared common helpers retain address-based names when not yet semantically named, such as ftCo_800CD1BC and ft_800CD31C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched functions are simple direct-call wrappers to common routines, which helps reproduce the exact small function bodies expected by the original binary.
For Coll wrappers, matching required emitting a call with both the GObj argument and the ft_800CD31C function pointer passed to ftCo_800CD2C4.
The bot report confirmed 8 new 100% matches: four Lipstick swing callbacks and four Star Rod swing callbacks.
The GALE01 report measured matched code at 36.21%, an increase of +0.01% and +272 bytes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

decomp.dev reported 8 new matches: ftCo_StarRodSwing_Coll, ftCo_LipstickSwing_Coll, ftCo_StarRodSwing_Anim, ftCo_StarRodSwing_IASA, ftCo_StarRodSwing_Phys, ftCo_LipstickSwing_Anim, ftCo_LipstickSwing_IASA, and ftCo_LipstickSwing_Phys.
The automated report showed each newly matched function moving from 0.00% before to 100.00% after.
follow_up_queries: Find other empty ft*Swing.c files whose Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks may forward to ftCo_800CD1BC, ftCo_800CD204, ftCo_800CD278, and ftCo_800CD2C4.
Audit remaining UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations in ft_0CD1.h for concrete ftCo_GObj* or callback signatures.
Compare SwordSwing, LipstickSwing, and StarRodSwing callback implementations for reusable naming and matching patterns.
